dominant_error_pattern: Students lack conceptual clarity on the taxonomy of engagement types, confusing 'social' (external interpersonal context) with the three evidence-based dimensions: cognitive, emotional, and behavioral engagement.
instructional_red_flags: Distractor D (Social): Student chose the *not* option correctly but likely by elimination or misunderstanding the dimension names. Teach the three pillars explicitly: Cognitive = mental effort/focus; Emotional = interest/belonging; Behavioral = participation/effort. Use a case: a student attending class (behavioral) but daydreaming (low cognitive) and feeling isolated (low emotional). Clarify: social is the *context*, not a dimension.

dominant_error_pattern: Students identify proximal school-level variables (motivation, climate, policies) but fail to recognize family involvement as a documented, independent predictor of student success separate from socioeconomic status and other demographic factors.
instructional_red_flags: Distractor B (Socioeconomic status): Student chose a demographic descriptor instead of a modifiable family-engagement variable. Teach: SES ≠ involvement. Families at all SES levels can engage meaningfully when barriers are addressed. Distractor C (Gender) and D (Curriculum): Student picked non-factors. Emphasize: family involvement is one of the *few* variables that research shows school psychologists can actively influence. Highlight the actionable lever.
